title: GitLab stock rating downgraded by Cantor Fitzgerald amid competition concerns
text: Cantor Fitzgerald analyst Jonathan Ruykhaver reduced GitLab's rating from Overweight to Neutral and slashed the price target significantly from $60 to $40, a 33% reduction. The analyst cited intensifying competition from GitHub and Atlassian, slowing SMB and public sector demand, and declining DBNRR. GitLab's stock has lost approximately 40% of its value over the past year and currently trades at $36.50, near its 52-week low. Despite competitive pressures, the company maintains strong gross profit margins of 88%.
